heading: Art. 109a. Mail matter: wrongful taking, opening, etc.
label: 909a Art. 109a. Mail matter: wrongful taking, opening, etc.
notes: Effective DateSection effective on , as designated by the President, with implementing regulations and provisions relating to applicability to various situations, see and Ex. Ord. No. 13825, set out as notes under .Jan. 1, 2019section 5542 of Pub. L. 114–328section 801 of this title

children.heading: .—Taking
children.label: a .—Taking
children.text: Any person subject to this chapter who, with the intent to obstruct the correspondence of, or to pry into the business or secrets of, any person or organization, wrongfully takes mail matter before the mail matter is delivered to or received by the addressee shall be punished as a court-martial may direct.

children.heading: .—Opening, Secreting, Destroying, Stealing
children.label: b .—Opening, Secreting, Destroying, Stealing
children.text: Any person subject to this chapter who wrongfully opens, secretes, destroys, or steals mail matter before the mail matter is delivered to or received by the addressee shall be punished as a court-martial may direct.
